items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Renewed Hormuz attacks & Samsung-led equity slide
items.summary: More - Samsung's record profit fails to stop shares plunging, dragging KOSPI down 6% Nasdaq analysis today at investingLive.com as Asian session shows tech under pressure Asia stocks fall as chip selloff spreads, breaking ranks with Wall St highs Softer Japan wage data unlikely to derail BOJ hikes, even as yen risk grows PBOC's Pan lifts Southbound Bond Connect quota to 800bn as Hong Kong expands gold links Chinese economist reportedly detained after pessimistic lecture, accounts wiped PBOC sets USD/ CNY central rate at 6.8054 (vs. estimate at 6.7838) More on reports Iranian forces strike LNG tanker and commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz South Korea's Kospi slammed 4% lower at open, Samsung slide Japan data, May 2026: Wage miss but growth solid, Household spending better than expected Reports of a ship attacked, Oman, Strait of Hormuz New Zealand dollar gains seen limited even if RBNZ hikes this week MUFG expects EUR/USD to recover toward 1.1800 as confidence improves US equity index futures have leapt higher at the Globex reopen - lower inflation? Fed's Waller and Warsh diverge over role of forward guidance. Public split at the Fed? ECB's Schnabel warns on inflation: Iran conflict shock still not over despite oil drop Turf war between US agencies threatens Trump's strategic Bitcoin reserve plan. BTC/USD up. investingLive Americas FX news wrap 6 Jul: Dow Hits Record, USD and yields mixed Trump says Walmart will lower the price of ground beef. (ps. Be wary of Trump's untruths) ICYMI, MORE OIL SUPPLY! - Canada finds faster way to pump more oil, Alberta drillers pivot Summary: Iran's military fired at least two missiles at commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz on Monday night, according to U.S. officials, after a one-week de-escalation agreement expired A loaded LNG tanker was reported struck and set ablaze east of Musandam, though the identity of the vessel remains unconfirmed The attacks put the recent U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding at risk and raise the likelihood of U.S. retaliation against Iranian targets Oil prices firmed modestly while gold eased back from a two-week high as investors awaited the Fed's June minutes KOSPI led regional equity losses as Samsung shares slumped despite a strong profit beat, with memory peers Micron and Sandisk also falling after-hours USD/JPY slipped back under 162.00 while other major currency pairs stayed rangebound President Trump said Walmart had agreed to lower prices on many products, adding to expectations of easing inflation pressure Renewed violence in the Strait of Hormuz overshadowed the session, after Iran's military fired at least two missiles at commercial vessels transiting the waterway on Monday night, according to U.S. officials cited by Axios and N12. The reported attack came just after a one-week agreement between Washington and Tehran to halt strikes in the strait had lapsed, raising the risk that the broader memorandum of understanding signed less than three weeks ago could unravel entirely. Britain's maritime trade operation said it had received a report of a tanker travelling south near the Omani coast being struck by an unknown projectile, sparking a fire, while a separate commercial vessel was also hit by a missile. Both ships suffered significant damage but there were no reported casualties. A loaded LNG tanker was also reported struck and set ablaze east of Musandam, though this detail remains unconfirmed. Talks between the U.S. and Iran in Doha last week had already ended with little progress on the strait issue, and markets are now bracing for a possible U.S. response. Against that backdrop, oil prices firmed modestly, while gold eased back from a two-week high as investors turned their attention to the Federal Reserve's June meeting minutes for clues on new Chair Kevin Warsh's policy approach. Equity markets told their own story, with the KOSPI the clear regional underperformer following an 8.2% slide in Samsung shares. The move came despite Samsung's preliminary second-quarter operating profit beating estimates, even as revenue landed in the middle of the analyst range and the outlook included provisions for employee bonuses equivalent to 10.5% of business performance earnings. Some analysts pointed to increased volatility from leveraged ETFs as a factor behind the scale of the selloff, though that explanation is worth treating with some caution given how quickly narratives around Samsung and the memory cycle have shifted recently. The Shanghai Composite and Hang Seng were also softer, though by less than the Nikkei and KOSPI, while US equity futures traded mixed, with the Nasdaq underperforming. Memory names Sandisk and Micron both fell after-hours in the wake of Samsung's figures. In currencies, USD/JPY dropped back under 162.00, while other major pairs remained rangebound. Adding a modestly disinflationary note to the session, President Trump said in a social media post that Walmart had agreed to lower prices on many of its products, a development likely to further temper expectations for near-term Fed rate hikes. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 更多——三星创纪录的利润未能阻止股价暴跌，拖累KOSPI指数下跌6%；今日在investingLive.com上发布纳斯达克市场分析，亚洲交易时段显示科技股承压；随着芯片抛售潮蔓延，亚洲股市下跌，与华尔街创下的高点背道而驰；日本疲软的工资数据不太可能阻碍日本央行加息， 即便日元风险加剧 中国人民银行行长潘将“南向债券通”额度上调至8000亿，香港同时扩大黄金联通 据报道，一名中国经济学家因发表悲观演讲被拘留，其社交媒体账号遭清空 中国人民银行将美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8054（市场预期为6.7838） 更多报道：伊朗部队在霍尔木兹海峡袭击液化天然气运输船和商船 韩国KOSPI指数开盘重挫4%，三星股价下跌 日本数据，2026年5月： 薪资数据不及预期但增长稳健，家庭支出好于预期 据报道，阿曼霍尔木兹海峡发生船只遇袭事件 即使新西兰央行本周加息，新西兰元升值空间仍有限 三菱日联金融集团预计，随着市场信心改善，欧元兑美元将回升至1.1800附近 美国股指期货在Globex重新开盘后大幅跳高——通胀放缓？ 美联储的沃勒和沃什在前瞻性指引的作用上存在分歧。 美联储内部出现分歧？欧洲央行施纳贝尔就通胀发出警告：尽管油价下跌，伊朗冲突带来的冲击尚未结束。美国各机构之间的权力之争威胁到特朗普的比特币战略储备计划。BTC/USD上涨。investingLive美洲外汇新闻综述 7月6日：道指创历史新高，美元和收益率涨跌不一 特朗普称沃尔玛将下调碎牛肉价格。（附注：请警惕特朗普的谎言） 错过了吗？原油供应进一步增加！——加拿大找到更快增产方式，阿尔伯塔省钻井商调整策略 摘要： 据美国官员称，在为期一周的缓和协议到期后，伊朗军方于周一晚间向穿越霍尔木兹海峡的商船发射了至少两枚导弹。据报道，一艘满载液化天然气的油轮在穆桑达姆以东遭到袭击并起火，但该船的身份尚未得到确认。此次袭击使近期美伊达成的谅解备忘录面临风险，并增加了美国 针对伊朗目标的报复行动。油价小幅走高，而黄金价格则从两周高点回落，投资者正等待美联储6月会议纪要。受三星股价暴跌拖累，尽管其盈利大幅超出预期，韩国综合股价指数（KOSPI）仍领跌亚股，同属存储芯片行业的美光和闪迪也随之下跌，此前 盘后美元兑日元回落至162.00下方，其他主要货币对则维持区间震荡。特朗普总统表示沃尔玛已同意下调许多产品的价格，这进一步增强了通胀压力将有所缓解的预期。据Axios和N12援引美国官员的话称，周一晚间伊朗军方向途经霍尔木兹海峡的商船发射了至少两枚导弹，该海峡再度爆发的暴力事件笼罩了当天的交易。据报道，此次袭击发生在华盛顿与德黑兰就暂停海峡袭击达成的为期一周的协议刚一到期之后，这增加了不到三周前签署的更广泛谅解备忘录可能完全破裂的风险。 英国海事贸易部门表示，已接到报告称，一艘在阿曼海岸附近向南航行的油轮遭到不明弹体击中并引发火灾，同时另一艘商船也遭到导弹袭击。两艘船只均遭受严重损坏，但据报无人员伤亡。另有报道称，一艘满载液化天然气的油轮在穆桑达姆以东海域遭到袭击并起火，但这一细节尚未得到证实。上周在美国多哈举行的美伊谈判已告结束，但在海峡问题上进展甚微，市场目前正为美国可能采取的应对措施做准备。在此背景下，油价小幅走强，而黄金价格则从两周高点回落，因为投资者将注意力转向美联储6月会议纪要，以寻找新任主席凯文·沃什的政策走向线索。股市表现各异，受三星股价暴跌8.2%拖累，韩国KOSPI指数成为该地区表现最差的股指。 尽管三星第二季度初步营业利润超出预期，且营收处于分析师预测区间的的中间位置，展望中还计入了相当于业务业绩收益10.5%的员工奖金拨备，但该公司股价仍出现下跌。一些分析师指出，杠杆ETF带来的波动性加剧是此次抛售规模较大的原因之一，但鉴于近期围绕三星和内存周期的市场叙事转变之快，这一解释值得谨慎对待。上证综合指数和恒生指数也出现回落，但跌幅小于日经指数和韩国KOSPI指数；美国股指期货涨跌不一，其中纳斯达克指数表现相对疲软。受三星财报影响，存储股闪迪和美光在盘后交易中均出现下跌。外汇市场上，美元兑日元回落至162.00下方，其他主要货币对则维持区间震荡。特朗普总统在社交媒体上发文称，沃尔玛已同意下调许多产品的价格，这一消息为当日市场增添了一丝温和的通缩基调，此举可能进一步抑制市场对美联储近期加息的预期。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。
items.summaryEn: More - Samsung's record profit fails to stop shares plunging, dragging KOSPI down 6% Nasdaq analysis today at investingLive.com as Asian session shows tech under pressure Asia stocks fall as chip selloff spreads, breaking ranks with Wall St highs Softer Japan wage data unlikely to derail BOJ hikes, even as yen risk grows PBOC's Pan lifts Southbound Bond Connect quota to 800bn as Hong Kong expands gold links Chinese economist reportedly detained after pessimistic lecture, accounts wiped PBOC sets USD/ CNY central rate at 6.8054 (vs. estimate at 6.7838) More on reports Iranian forces strike LNG tanker and commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz South Korea's Kospi slammed 4% lower at open, Samsung slide Japan data, May 2026: Wage miss but growth solid, Household spending better than expected Reports of a ship attacked, Oman, Strait of Hormuz New Zealand dollar gains seen limited even if RBNZ hikes this week MUFG expects EUR/USD to recover toward 1.1800 as confidence improves US equity index futures have leapt higher at the Globex reopen - lower inflation? Be wary of Trump's untruths) ICYMI, MORE OIL SUPPLY! - Canada finds faster way to pump more oil, Alberta drillers pivot Summary: Iran's military fired at least two missiles at commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz on Monday night, according to U.S. officials, after a one-week de-escalation agreement expired A loaded LNG tanker was reported struck and set ablaze east of Musandam, though the identity of the vessel remains unconfirmed The attacks put the recent U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding at risk and raise the likelihood of U.S. retaliation against Iranian targets Oil prices firmed modestly while gold eased back from a two-week high as investors awaited the Fed's June minutes KOSPI led regional equity losses as Samsung shares slumped despite a strong profit beat, with memory peers Micron and Sandisk also falling after-hours USD/JPY slipped back under 162.00 while other major currency pairs stayed rangebound President Trump said Walmart had agreed to lower prices on many products, adding to expectations of easing inflation pressure Renewed violence in the Strait of Hormuz overshadowed the session, after Iran's military fired at least two missiles at commercial vessels transiting the waterway on Monday night, according to U.S. officials cited by Axios and N12. The reported attack came just after a one-week agreement between Washington and Tehran to halt strikes in the strait had lapsed, raising the risk that the broader memorandum of understanding signed less than three weeks ago could unravel entirely. Britain's maritime trade operation said it had received a report of a tanker travelling south near the Omani coast being struck by an unknown projectile, sparking a fire, while a separate commercial vessel was also hit by a missile. Both ships suffered significant damage but there were no reported casualties. A loaded LNG tanker was also reported struck and set ablaze east of Musandam, though this detail remains unconfirmed. Talks between the U.S. and Iran in Doha last week had already ended with little progress on the strait issue, and markets are now bracing for a possible U.S. response. Against that backdrop, oil prices firmed modestly, while gold eased back from a two-week high as investors turned their attention to the Federal Reserve's June meeting minutes for clues on new Chair Kevin Warsh's policy approach. Equity markets told their own story, with the KOSPI the clear regional underperformer following an 8.2% slide in Samsung shares. The move came despite Samsung's preliminary second-quarter operating profit beating estimates, even as revenue landed in the middle of the analyst range and the outlook included provisions for employee bonuses equivalent to 10.5% of business performance earnings. Some analysts pointed to increased volatility from leveraged ETFs as a factor behind the scale of the selloff, though that explanation is worth treating with some caution given how quickly narratives around Samsung and the memory cycle have shifted recently. The Shanghai Composite and Hang Seng were also softer, though by less than the Nikkei and KOSPI, while US equity futures traded mixed, with the Nasdaq underperforming. Memory names Sandisk and Micron both fell after-hours in the wake of Samsung's figures. In currencies, USD/JPY dropped back under 162.00, while other major pairs remained rangebound. Adding a modestly disinflationary note to the session, President Trump said in a social media post that Walmart had agreed to lower prices on many of its products, a development likely to further temper expectations for near-term Fed rate hikes. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 6 Jul: Dow Hits Record, USD and yields mixed
items.summary: US stocks close higher. Another record for the Dow. Nasdaq is the best performing index Crude oil settles down $0.14 at $68.55 German Dax/Italy FTSE MIB close at a new records, but other indices close lower. ECBs Wunsch: it seems that Iran shop has disappeared. Have not seen much 2nd round effects More from Waller: Fed policymakers have always been committed to 2% inflation ECB Schnabel: Current price shock cannot simply be looked through. Fed;s Waller: Forward guidance can be a valuable tool that has strengthened policymaking US ISM Non-Manufacturing PMI for June 54.0 vs 54.0 estimate Canada S&P global services PMI index for June 47.1 vs 50.6 in May The US dollar starts the North American trading week on firmer footing investingLive European markets wrap: Dollar firms, US stocks eye bounce to start the week The U.S. dollar finished the day mixed vs the major currencies. The greenback was higher against 4 of the seven major currencies, posting its largest gain against the JPY (+0.45%). It rose vs the CHF by +0.25%), and the NZD by 0.12%. It eked out a small 0.04% rise vs the CAD (+0.04%). On the downside, the greenback lost ground on the day vs the EUR -0.03% GBP -0.26% AUD +0.22% In the equity markets, the U.S. stocks kicked off the week on a strong note, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average closing at another record high while the Nasdaq led the major indices thanks to renewed strength in large-cap technology and semiconductor shares. The S&P 500 also finished solidly higher as investors returned from the holiday weekend in a risk-on mood. The rally came despite mixed market breadth, with leadership concentrated in AI and chip-related names. On the economic front, the ISM Services PMI came in exactly as expected at 54.0, signaling that the U.S. service sector continues to expand at a healthy pace. While the headline matched forecasts, the details painted a constructive picture of the economy with business activity remaining resilient despite ongoing concerns over tariffs, fuel prices, and geopolitical uncertainty. The report reinforced the view that the U.S. economy continues to grow at a moderate pace. Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller spoke on a panel from Italy and focused on monetary policy and communication. Waller defended the use of forward guidance, calling it a valuable tool that has strengthened monetary policymaking when used appropriately. At the same time, he emphasized that the Fed's commitment to its 2% inflation target has never changed, pushing back against suggestions that policymakers have become less focused on price stability. His comments suggested the Fed remains committed to transparency while maintaining credibility in its inflation-fighting framework. Markets showed little reaction to Waller's remarks, with investors instead focusing on the broader earnings outlook and continued enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence-related stocks. This Treasury yields were mixed with the shorter end lower and the longer end higher: 2 year yield 4.112%, -1.9 basis points 5 year yield 4.202%, -1.9 basis points 10 year yield 4.471%, -0.7 basis points 30 year yield 4.986%, +0.5 basis points. Highlights Dow closes at another record high, extending its recent run. Nasdaq leads gains as semiconductor and AI stocks rebound sharply. ISM Services PMI: 54.0 vs. 54.0 expected, pointing to continued expansion in the U.S. services sector. Fed's Waller: Forward guidance remains an important policy tool when used correctly. Fed's Waller: Policymakers remain fully committed to achieving and maintaining 2% inflation. Investors now turn their attention to this week's FOMC minutes and the unofficial start of second-quarter earnings season for the next major catalysts. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 美股收高。道指再创历史新高。纳斯达克指数表现最佳。原油结算价下跌0.14美元，报68.55美元。德国DAX指数和意大利FTSE MIB指数收于历史新高，但其他指数收低。欧洲央行（ECB）的温施（Wunsch）表示：伊朗市场似乎已不复存在。尚未看到明显的二轮效应。沃勒补充道：美联储决策者一直致力于实现2%的通胀目标。欧洲央行施纳贝尔表示：当前的价格冲击不能简单地置之不理。美联储的沃勒：前瞻性指引可以成为一项有价值的工具，它强化了政策制定。美国6月ISM非制造业PMI为54.0，符合54.0的预期；加拿大6月标普全球服务业PMI指数为47.1，低于5月的50.6。美元在北美交易周伊始走强。investingLive欧洲市场综述： 美元走强，美股本周初有望反弹 美元兑主要货币收盘涨跌不一。美元兑七种主要货币中的四种走高，其中对日元涨幅最大（+0.45%）。美元兑瑞士法郎上涨0.25%，兑新西兰元上涨0.12%。美元兑加元小幅上涨0.04%（+0.04%）。 另一方面，美元当日兑欧元下跌0.03%，兑英镑下跌0.26%，兑澳元上涨0.22%。在股市方面，美国股市本周开局强劲，道琼斯工业平均指数收于又一历史高点，而纳斯达克指数则在大型科技股和半导体股再度走强带动下领涨主要股指。标普500指数也收盘大幅走高，因投资者在长周末假期后回归市场，风险偏好情绪高涨。尽管市场广度表现参差不齐，但此次上涨主要由人工智能和芯片相关个股领涨。在经济方面，ISM服务业PMI数据与预期完全一致，报54.0，表明美国服务业继续以稳健的步伐扩张。虽然总数据符合预期，但报告细节描绘了一幅积极的经济图景：尽管关税、燃料价格以及地缘政治不确定性等担忧持续存在，商业活动仍保持韧性。该报告强化了美国经济将继续以温和速度增长的观点。美联储理事克里斯托弗·沃勒在意大利参加了一场小组讨论，重点谈及货币政策和沟通问题。 沃勒为使用前瞻性指引进行了辩护，称其是一种有价值的工具，只要使用得当，就能加强货币政策制定。与此同时，他强调美联储对2%通胀目标的承诺从未改变，驳斥了有关决策者对价格稳定关注度降低的说法。他的言论表明，美联储在坚持透明度的同时，仍致力于维护其抗通胀框架的可信度。市场对沃勒的言论反应平淡，投资者反而将注意力集中在更广泛的盈利前景以及人工智能相关股票持续高涨的热潮上。国债收益率涨跌不一，短期收益率走低，长期收益率走高：2年期收益率4.112%，下跌1.9个基点；5年期收益率4.202%，下跌1.9个基点；10年期收益率4.471%，下跌0.7个基点；30年期收益率4.986%，上涨0.5个基点。市场亮点：道琼斯指数收于又一历史高点，延续了近期涨势。纳斯达克指数领涨，半导体和人工智能类股强劲反弹。ISM服务业PMI：54.0，符合54.0的预期，表明美国服务业持续扩张。 美联储委员沃勒表示：如果使用得当，前瞻性指引仍是一项重要的政策工具。美联储委员沃勒表示：决策者仍完全致力于实现并维持2%的通胀目标。投资者目前将目光转向本周的联邦公开市场委员会（FOMC）会议纪要以及第二季度财报季的非正式开局，以寻找下一个重要催化因素。本文作者为investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基。
items.summaryEn: US stocks close higher. Another record for the Dow. Nasdaq is the best performing index Crude oil settles down $0.14 at $68.55 German Dax/Italy FTSE MIB close at a new records, but other indices close lower. ECBs Wunsch: it seems that Iran shop has disappeared. Have not seen much 2nd round effects More from Waller: Fed policymakers have always been committed to 2% inflation ECB Schnabel: Current price shock cannot simply be looked through. On the economic front, the ISM Services PMI came in exactly as expected at 54.0, signaling that the U.S. service sector continues to expand at a healthy pace. While the headline matched forecasts, the details painted a constructive picture of the economy with business activity remaining resilient despite ongoing concerns over tariffs, fuel prices, and geopolitical uncertainty. The report reinforced the view that the U.S. economy continues to grow at a moderate pace. Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller spoke on a panel from Italy and focused on monetary policy and communication. Waller defended the use of forward guidance, calling it a valuable tool that has strengthened monetary policymaking when used appropriately. At the same time, he emphasized that the Fed's commitment to its 2% inflation target has never changed, pushing back against suggestions that policymakers have become less focused on price stability. His comments suggested the Fed remains committed to transparency while maintaining credibility in its inflation-fighting framework. Markets showed little reaction to Waller's remarks, with investors instead focusing on the broader earnings outlook and continued enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence-related stocks. This Treasury yields were mixed with the shorter end lower and the longer end higher: 2 year yield 4.112%, -1.9 basis points 5 year yield 4.202%, -1.9 basis points 10 year yield 4.471%, -0.7 basis points 30 year yield 4.986%, +0.5 basis points. Highlights Dow closes at another record high, extending its recent run. Nasdaq leads gains as semiconductor and AI stocks rebound sharply. ISM Services PMI: 54.0 vs. 54.0 expected, pointing to continued expansion in the U.S. services sector. Fed's Waller: Forward guidance remains an important policy tool when used correctly. Fed's Waller: Policymakers remain fully committed to achieving and maintaining 2% inflation. Investors now turn their attention to this week's FOMC minutes and the unofficial start of second-quarter earnings season for the next major catalysts. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the Eurozone Retail Sales and PPI data that won't change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will likely be muted. The markets are mostly in consolidation mode as we await the US CPI report due next Tuesday. That should influence interest rate expectations more than the recent US NFP report. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, we get the US ISM Services PMI which is expected to ease to 54.0 vs 54.5 prior. The S&P Global US PMIs showed a small uptick in services but the agency noted that "the services sector continues to grow at an especially subdued pace, reflecting push-back from customers over high prices amid low levels of consumer confidence in particular". The agency also added that "input cost inflation has shown sign of cooling in June thanks in part to the lower energy prices seen at the tail end of the survey data collection period”. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 15:00 GMT/11:00 ET - Fed's Waller (neutral - voter) 15:00 GMT/11:00 ET - ECB's Schnabel (hawkish - voter) 16:00 GMT/12:00 ET - ECB President Lagarde (neutral - voter) 16:45 GMT/12:45 ET - BoE's Mann (neutral - voter) 18:30 GMT/14:30 ET - ECB's Lane (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，除了欧元区零售销售和生产者物价指数（PPI）等几项低关注度的数据外，没有太多值得关注的内容，这些数据不会对欧洲央行的政策产生影响，因此市场反应预计将较为温和。在等待下周二即将公布的美国CPI报告期间，市场大多处于盘整状态。该报告对利率预期的影响应会大于近期公布的美国非农就业报告。 美国时段 在美国时段，我们将迎来美国ISM服务业PMI数据，预计该数据将从前值54.5小幅回落至54.0。标普全球美国PMI数据显示服务业略有回升，但该机构指出：“服务业仍以尤为温和的步伐增长，这反映出在消费者信心普遍低迷的背景下，客户对高价的抵触情绪。”该机构还补充道：“6月份投入成本通胀已显现降温迹象，部分原因在于调查数据收集期尾声阶段能源价格有所回落。” 央行官员讲话 格林尼治标准时间15:00/美国东部时间11:00 - 美联储的沃勒（中性 - 有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间15:00/美国东部时间11:00 - 欧洲央行的施纳贝尔（鹰派 - 有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间16:00/美国东部时间12:00 - 欧洲央行行长拉加德（中性——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间 16:45/美国东部时间 12:45 - 英格兰银行的曼恩（中性——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间 18:30/美国东部时间 14:30 - 欧洲央行的莱恩（中性——有投票权） 本文作者为 investinglive.com 的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔。

The markets are mostly in consolidation mode as we await the US CPI report due next Tuesday. That should influence interest rate expectations more than the recent US NFP report. The S&P Global US PMIs showed a small uptick in services but the agency noted that "the services sector continues to grow at an especially subdued pace, reflecting push-back from customers over high prices amid low levels of consumer confidence in particular". The agency also added that "input cost inflation has shown sign of cooling in June thanks in part to the lower energy prices seen at the tail end of the survey data collection period”.
